entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A grain crop evaluated for genotypic resistance to the head bug.

entities.name: CALOCORIS ANGUSTATUS
entities.description: The sorghum head bug, a significant pest that causes grain damage.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes to withstand or minimize damage from Calocoris angustatus.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating germplasm accessions to identify resistant genotypes.

entities.name: NATURAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental settings where the crop is exposed to pests naturally.

entities.name: HEADCAGE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled settings where pests are artificially introduced to the crop for testing.

entities.name: BUG NUMBERS
entities.description: The count of bugs per panicle used as a metric for resistance.

entities.name: GRAIN DAMAGE
entities.description: The level of damage to the grain, rated on a scale from 1 to 5.

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: The percentage of seeds that germinate following pest infestation.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific climatic period during which screening was conducted.

entities.name: GERMPLASM ACCESSIONS
entities.description: The collection of genetic materials screened for resistance.
